text: Mobile app development involves creating software for smartphones and tablets. A key principle is responsive design, which ensures that apps display well on different screen sizes and orientations. Developers must decide between building native apps, which are created specifically for one platform like iOS or Android, or cross-platform apps, which work on multiple systems using shared code. Native apps typically offer better performance and deeper access to device features, while cross-platform options reduce development time and cost. Once completed, apps are published through official app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store, where they undergo review. Performance is critical—users expect fast loading, smooth animations, and minimal battery drain.
